SNOW in the front with the pro-
gressive members.


And he is not the only one, al
belt, perhaps the most noticeable.
The senate, which a week ago was
anti-suffrage by a good majority
on a two thirds vote, has been
changing. In fact there has been
so much doing in the line of see
ing erroneous ways that it is hard
telling where it will stop. The
suffragists have not yet won their
fight by any means, but they have
gained enough ground to give them
more than an even chance. Even
those senators who are constitu-
tionally opposed to suffrage or any
thing that resembles it now admit
their position is weakening.


Every day finds fresh addition
to the suffragette lobby until now
it is difficult from a glance in the
capitol corridor to tell whether
there is legislature in session or
a woman's book club. Woman suf-
frage is decidedly above par just
now
